title: Chromatic Damage Lab
description: A post-processing laboratory where the DispersionTest prism refracts light onto the DamagedHelmet, and the user sculpts a chain of effects including bloom, chromatic aberration, vignette, film grain, and tone mapping. Each effect has coupled intensity parameters governed by an energy conservation law — total post-process energy is capped, so boosting one effect forces others to redistribute. Full undo/redo history tracks every adjustment.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with the postprocessing pipeline from three/examples/jsm. Load DispersionTest.glb on the left and DamagedHelmet.glb on the right of the scene, separated by ~3 units. Add a SpotLight aimed from the DispersionTest toward the DamagedHelmet to simulate refracted light hitting the helmet. Use a PerspectiveCamera at (0,1.5,5) with OrbitControls. Build an EffectComposer with these five passes: UnrealBloomPass, ShaderPass for chromatic aberration (custom RGB offset shader), ShaderPass for vignette (radial darkening shader), ShaderPass for film grain (animated noise shader), and an ACESFilmicToneMapping output pass with adjustable exposure. Create a dat.GUI panel with sliders for each effect's intensity (0-100). Implement an energy conservation system: total summed intensity across all five effects must not exceed 250. When the user raises one slider, if the total would exceed 250, proportionally scale down the other four sliders to compensate, updating the GUI display in real time. Each slider change is a discrete step. Add two buttons: Undo and Redo, maintaining a history stack of slider state snapshots (arrays of five values). Undo restores the previous state and pushes current to redo stack; Redo reverses this. The chromatic aberration pass should visually shift RGB channels proportionally to its intensity. The vignette radius and film grain amount should respond to their respective sliders. Bloom threshold and strength map from the bloom slider. Exposure maps from the tone mapping slider. Display a small HUD text showing current total energy out of 250. Ensure both models are visible and lit so the post-process effects are clearly observable on both assets simultaneously. The interaction requires 10-14 steps of adjusting sliders, observing redistribution, and using undo/redo to explore different post-process combinations.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bloomIntensity: number,         // current bloom pass intensity (0-100)
  chromaticIntensity: number,         // current chromatic aberration intensity (0-100)
  vignetteIntensity: number,         // current vignette intensity (0-100)
  grainIntensity: number,         // current film grain intensity (0-100)
  toneMappingExposure: number,         // current tone mapping exposure intensity (0-100)
  totalIntensity: number,         // sum of all five effect intensities
  historyStackDepth: number,         // count of states in undo history stack
  redoStackDepth: number,         // count of states in redo history stack
  userInteractionCount: number,         // total slider adjustments made by user
  energyConservationTriggered: boolean,         // whether last slider change triggered proportional scaling
  modelsLoaded: number,         // count of GLB models successfully loaded (0-2)
  composerPassCount: number,         // number of active post-processing passes in EffectComposer
}
